description_long: This is a compact, curved-blade knife resembling a karambit, featuring a sharply hooked, single-edged blade with a matte black finish. The blade has a pronounced curve and a pointed tip, optimized for slicing or hooking motions. The handle is ergonomically shaped with finger grooves and a prominent ring at the end, allowing for secure grip and versatile handling. The handle also features cut-out sections and a visible metallic pivot or fastener, suggesting a folding or fixed mechanism. The overall design is tactical and modern, with no visible ornamentation or decorative elements. The materials appear to be primarily metal, likely steel, with some synthetic or composite elements in the handle.
description: A compact, curved karambit-style knife with a matte black blade and ergonomic handle featuring a finger ring and cut-out sections. The design is tactical and modern, with a visible metallic fastener and no ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curve of the blade, finger ring, and handle cut-outs.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and handle profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade curve, handle ring, and fastener from the other side.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite direction, emphasizing the slim profile and handle structure.
attribute_handle_design: ergonomic with finger grooves, cut-out sections, and a finger ring; likely composite or metal
